explanation: Deep descending bass notes. Bass Drops.

discriminators: Defined by a monotonically FALLING trajectory: centroid slope and pitch slope are strongly negative. This is the sign-flip of DESIGNED-RISER, which is otherwise the same morphology.
Energy migrates into the sub-200 Hz band as the sound proceeds; DESIGNED-RUMBLE is low-band too but is stationary with no trajectory.
Longer and more continuous than DESIGNED-BOOM, which is a single front-loaded transient, not a glide.

explanation: big trailer deep hits. LFE type low end sweeteners.

provenance.policy: mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened
mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened

discriminators: 'Deep short and tight' — a single LF-dominated transient with a fast decay. Shorter and darker than DESIGNED-IMPACT, and far more front-loaded than DESIGNED-RUMBLE.
Unlike DESIGNED-BASS DIVE there is no pitch/centroid trajectory: the spectrum is fixed and the envelope does the work.
Darker than DESIGNED-STINGER, which carries substantial high-band content.

explanation: Long deep horn like sounds. Like a War of the World's type long sound
or like a long french horn braam type sound.

discriminators: A long, LOW, strongly harmonic held note — a horn, not a hit. Harmonicity separates it from DESIGNED-RUMBLE (noise) and its length separates it from DESIGNED-BOOM (impulse).
Pitched and stable where DESIGNED-BASS DIVE glides downward.
Lower fundamental and more brass-like partial structure than DESIGNED-DRONE, and with a defined swell rather than an unchanging bed.

explanation: Particulary distored designed sounds and distortion elements.

discriminators: The squashed signature: high total_harmonic_distortion and clipping_density with an ABNORMALLY LOW crest factor — the waveform has been driven into a wall, so peak/RMS collapses toward unity.
Low crest is what separates it from every other loud designed sound (BOOM, IMPACT, STINGER all have high crest).
Noisier and harmonically fouler than DESIGNED-SYNTHETIC; less periodic than DESIGNED-RHYTHMIC.

explanation: long stylized sounds with not much activity or movement.

discriminators: The textbook stationary case: 'long stylized sounds with not much activity or movement'. Near-zero spectral_flux, stationarity approaching 1, long length, flat envelope, almost no onsets.
Movement is the discriminator: any appreciable centroid trajectory makes it RISER/BASS DIVE; any churn makes it GRANULAR or MORPH; any onset train makes it RHYTHMIC.
DESIGNED-EERIE is the same family but restless — higher flux, lower stationarity, more inharmonic.

explanation: but tense strange undefined sounds that are eerie
suspenseful. Some overlap with DESIGNED-DRONE possible.

discriminators: Explanation admits overlap with DESIGNED-DRONE. The measurable split: EERIE is a drone that MOVES — higher spectral_flux, lower stationarity, more centroid modulation, and markedly more inharmonic (detuned, unsettled partials).
Tension/dread is semantic; DSP can only narrow to 'long, unstable, inharmonic bed'. Require text agreement to commit.

explanation: Ethereal wispy designed sounds. Ghostlike whispers
or heavenly tones. Otherworldly. See also DESIGNED-EERIE.

discriminators: 'Wispy', 'airy', 'heavenly': the bright, low-energy, HF-weighted sibling of DESIGNED-EERIE. Centroid sits well above 2 kHz and low_band_energy is near zero — the opposite weighting to DRONE/RUMBLE.
Softer and less percussive than MAGIC-SHIMMER, which has a dense onset train of glassy transients; ETHEREAL is a smooth bed.

explanation: sounds that have a gritty textural type sound
sounds broken down into tiny slices and recompiled.

discriminators: 'Gritty textural... think PaulStretch': a DENSE, high-flux micro-event field. spectral_flux is the headline — grains re-scatter the spectrum every frame, so flux is an order of magnitude above DESIGNED-DRONE.
Dense but APERIODIC: high onset_rate with LOW onset_periodicity is exactly what separates it from DESIGNED-RHYTHMIC, whose onsets lock to a grid.
More eventful than DESIGNED-DRONE (flux ~0.35 vs ~0.03) and less harmonically fouled than DESIGNED-DISTORTION (grit is temporal, not clipped).

explanation: stylized big hits. Possible overlap with DESIGNED-STINGER.

discriminators: A big front-loaded hit: near-instant attack, temporal centroid early, high crest, then a designed tail. Explanation admits overlap with DESIGNED-STINGER; the honest split is spectral, not temporal — IMPACT is broadband/LF-weighted, STINGER is bright and piercing.
Longer and less exclusively sub-bass than DESIGNED-BOOM.
Not a sweep: no monotonic centroid trajectory (that would be BASS DIVE or WHOOSH).

explanation: Designed sounds not fitting another category in this list.

discriminators: Deliberate catch-all. No morphology commitment and no priors: this is the abstention target when the top posterior inside DESIGNED is low or the top two are within a hair of each other.
Never assert MISC from signal — it wins only by default.

'Something transitioning into another thing': the spectrum at the end does not resemble the spectrum at the start. Low stationarity plus high centroid DEVIATION, but WITHOUT the monotonic slope that defines RISER/BASS DIVE — a morph wanders, a sweep travels.
Fewer, coarser events than DESIGNED-GRANULAR (which is a dense micro-event field, not a slow crossfade of identities).

explanation: something similar to a rising string line to create tension.

discriminators: The cleanest sweep in UCS: spectral_centroid_slope_hz_per_second is strongly POSITIVE across the whole file, and the amplitude envelope is back-loaded (temporal centroid > 0.6, negative skew) because a riser crescendos into its own end.
The gate is the sign of the slope. A negative slope is DESIGNED-BASS DIVE — same morphology, opposite direction.
A WHOOSH rises then falls (large centroid deviation, near-zero net slope); a RISER only rises.

explanation: Designed rumbles for layering into a mix. Earthquake rumbles. Composite rumble.

discriminators: Sustained sub-bass NOISE bed. low_band_energy is dominant (~0.8) with essentially nothing above 2 kHz, and — unlike BASS DIVE — no trajectory at all.
Not front-loaded: DESIGNED-BOOM has the same spectral weighting but is an impulse with an early temporal centroid; RUMBLE holds.
Noisier and less pitched than DESIGNED-BRAAM or DESIGNED-DRONE.

explanation: Stattaco or glitchy machine type loops

discriminators: 'Staccato or glitchy machine type loops... things with defined starts, stops, rhythmic stutters.' The single decisive feature is onset_periodicity: the onset train autocorrelates strongly (a grid), and beats_per_minute is likely to fire non-zero.
DESIGNED-GRANULAR has an equally dense onset train but it is STOCHASTIC — high onset_rate, low onset_periodicity. Periodicity is the whole boundary.
Gated hard on transient_count: a one-shot cannot be rhythmic.

explanation: For recordings meant to be used as source for other designed sounds

discriminators: Defined by INTENT, not by sound: 'recordings meant to be used as source for other designed sounds, experimental recordings, elements'. A source file is whatever it was recorded from — any morphology is legal.
Excluded from signal scoring entirely (alpha = 0). Only filename/metadata text ('source', 'raw', 'element') can select it.

explanation: stylized screen sweeteners. Big trailer type sounds. Big shock scare type sounds. Stylized stings
hits and impacts. Overlap with DESIGNED-IMPACT.

discriminators: Explanation concedes overlap with DESIGNED-IMPACT; treat as narrowable, not separable. The measurable lean: a stinger is PIERCING — high_band_energy and centroid well above IMPACT's — and its designed reverb tail runs longer relative to the hit.
Far brighter than DESIGNED-BOOM, which is almost pure low band.
Text ('sting', 'stab', 'trailer', 'cinematic') must agree before committing.

explanation: very electronic synth stuff. Raw work files of synth-play.

discriminators: 'Very synthesized... raw work files of synth-play.' Signal evidence is weak because a synth can make ANY morphology; what it cannot easily fake is a clean, exactly-harmonic partial ladder with a low noise floor.
Overlaps DESIGNED-TONAL (which is far more strictly single-pitched and flatness-free) and DESIGNED-DISTORTION (which is defined by clipping, not by harmonic purity).
Lean on synonyms ('modular', 'analog', 'synth') to commit.

explanation: Designed sounds with a particularly tonal nature. The ringing in your ear of tinnitus for example.

discriminators: 'A particularly tonal nature. The ringing in your ear of tinnitus.' The two gates ARE the definition: harmonicity high and spectral_flatness near zero — a near-sinusoidal, stable-f0 signal.
Purer than DESIGNED-SYNTHETIC (which tolerates noise and distortion) and infinitely purer than DESIGNED-DRONE, which may be a noise bed with no f0 at all.
Held, so not BEEPS: a beep is a short gated tone in an onset train, this is a sustained ring.

explanation: reversed talking etc. Not for Creatures.

discriminators: Vocal-derived DESIGN: formant structure and 3-8 Hz syllabic modulation survive the processing, so voicing_ratio stays high even when the source is reversed or pitched.
The 'designed' half is semantic — VOICES-WHISPER and DESIGNED-VOCAL measure alike. Text and heavy effects (long tails, reversal, extreme pitch) are the tiebreak.
Explicitly NOT for Creatures: CREATURES-* vocalizations belong there.

explanation: definite possible overlap confusion with SHOOSHES-WHOOSH
but this for less natural and more stylized versions.

discriminators: Band-swept NOISE with a rise-fall envelope: high spectral_flatness (no f0), large spectral_centroid_deviation as the filter travels, but near-zero NET centroid slope because it comes back down. That rise-fall is the split against DESIGNED-RISER (monotonic up) and BASS DIVE (monotonic down).

Explanation concedes confusion with SWOOSHES-WHOOSH; this entry is for the LESS natural, MORE stylized versions — expect designed HF sheen, longer tails and layered sub content that a recorded swoosh lacks.
Not impulsive: attack is a slow swell (~0.3 s), not a click.
